mnemonics: Type is for 'Type Of' commands, Locate is for 'Location'
T is for Type, L is for Locate
keyPoints: The 'type' command is used to determine the type of a command.
The 'type' command can be used to locate a command in the shell's command history.
Knowing the type of a command can help you understand its intended use and behavior.
The 'type' command can be used in combination with the 'man' command to view a command's manual page.
Locating a command can help you understand its options and usage.
Using the 'type' command can save time and reduce errors.
The 'type' command can be used to verify that a command is available and functional.
The 'type' command can be used in scripts and automation to check for command availability.
